text: Calculate TDEE, eat in 400-500 kcal daily deficit with 1.8-2.2g protein per kg bodyweight, track intake accurately with app for 12-16 weeks until 10-12% body fat (visible abs, sharp jaw, no love handles).

Resistance train 4x per week (upper/lower or PPL split) emphasizing compound lifts (squat, deadlift variation, bench, OHP, rows, pullups) + 3x direct neck work (4x10-15 neck curls + extensions with harness or plate) + 2x lateral raises for shoulder width.
Walk 10,000+ steps daily (or equivalent zone 2 cardio 4-5x/week 45min) while in deficit to preserve muscle and further reduce facial bloat/submental fat.
Train upper back, rear delts, and lats 2-3x/week with face pulls, band pullaparts, wide grip rows, pullups to build "cape" V-taper and improve shoulder-to-waist ratio.
Develop visible forearms and vascularity via 2-3x direct grip/farmers carries, reverse curls, wrist roller 3x10-20 + dead hangs.
Fix anterior pelvic tilt and forward head posture with daily 5-10min routine: glute bridges 3x15, hip flexor stretches 60s/side, chin tucks 3x10, wall angels, thoracic extensions on foam roller.
Build thick neck and traps to 16-17.5"+ circumference (for average height) via the neck work + heavy shrugs/farmers + direct trap bar shrugs 2x/week.
Bulk lean to add 8-15lbs muscle first (if under 15% BF and skinny fat or under-muscled), then cut; repeat 2-3 cycles if novice/intermediate.
Incorporate 2-3x/week direct core (hanging leg raises, ab wheel, cable crunches) and obliques (woodchoppers, side planks) for midsection aesthetics and waist control during cut.
Use 5-10g creatine monohydrate daily + 3-5g beta alanine if desired for performance/recovery during training phases; monitor water retention on face during cut.
Sleep in 16-19C dark room 7.5-9hrs consistent; use blackout, no screens 60min pre bed, magnesium threonate or glycinate 300mg.
Eliminate liquid calories, liquid oils in excess, and high-sodium processed foods during cut phases; time carbs around workouts.
For height presentation: wear quality 1-1.5" subtle lifts or thicker sole shoes/boots (e.g. certain chelsea or dress boots) that don't look orthopedic; pair with correct inseam pants.
Measure and track key ratios monthly: shoulder width, neck, waist at navel, BF estimate via photos/scale + tape.
If under-muscled and high BF ("skinny fat"), prioritize recomp or bulk first rather than aggressive cut; focus on strength gains 3-6 months.

Start prescription-strength tretinoin 0.025% or 0.05% nightly (pea size, after 20min wait post clean se, buffer with ceramide moisturizer first 4-8 weeks); apply SPF 50+ broad spectrum (mineral preferred) every morning reapply midday.
If moderate-severe acne or scarring, consult derm for 3-6 month Accutane (isotretinoin) course at 0.5-1mg/kg cumulative dose under supervision; follow with 6+ months tretinoin maintenance.
Double cleanse at night (oil/balm then gentle cleanser); use 10-15% vitamin C serum (L-ascorbic) in AM under moisturizer/SPF for brightness and pigmentation.
Microneedle face 1.0-1.5mm depth once every 4-6 weeks (sterile pen or stamp, not roller) + 0.5-1mm on scalp 1x/week; follow with hyaluronic or growth factor serum.
Apply daily facial moisturizer with ceramides, niacinamide 5%, and HA; body lotion immediately after shower while damp.
Strict sun avoidance + reapply SPF; add oral polypodium leucotomos or astaxanthin 12mg if high sun exposure for extra protection per some derm recs.
Gentle chemical exfoliation 1-2x week with 5-10% mandelic or lactic acid if not on tret; never over-exfoliate.
For under eye hollows/dark circles: consistent sleep + hydration + allergy management + topical caffeine/niacinamide eye cream; consider PRP or filler only after softmax exhausted.
Maintain short clean nails, filed, cuticles pushed; whiten teeth if yellowed (see dental section).
If oily or acne prone post workout: immediate water rinse + salicylic acid 2% spot treatment only as needed.
Use a silk or satin pillowcase and change pillowcase 2-3x/week to reduce acne mechanica and hair breakage.
Test for and fix vitamin D deficiency (<40-50 ng/ml) and low iron/ferritin via bloodwork; supplement D3 2000-5000IU + K2 + magnesium.
Avoid dairy (especially whey) and high glycemic foods if prone to hormonal acne; trial elimination 4-6 weeks.
Professional hydrafacial or chemical peel 1x/quarter for maintenance after initial 6mo home routine.
Stay consistently hydrated (3.5-5L water/day depending on size/climate); add electrolytes if training hard.
If Norwood 2+, start 1mg finasteride oral daily (or topical 0.1-0.25%) + 5% minoxidil foam/liquid 1ml twice daily on scalp; add 2.5mg oral minoxidil if tolerant (monitor BP, heart).
If advanced bald or poor responder, get FUE or FUT hair transplant with reputable surgeon (3000-4500 grafts typical for NW3-4); fin/minox lifelong to protect native and transplant.
Grow and maintain short neat stubble or full beard if density allows (use 5% minox on face 2x/day for 6-12mo if patchy, then maintain); line up and trim weekly with quality trimmer.

Get professional barber cut every 3-4 weeks; choose style based on face shape analysis (round: longer top textured, square: soft edges, long: volume sides etc); bring 3-5 reference photos of similar bone structure men.
Dye or fill eyebrows subtly darker if light/fair (use beard dye 3-5min or professional tint); pluck only stray monobrow hairs, never thin or arch dramatically.
If receding or high hairline, consider hair system (toupee/lace with glue or tape) professionally fitted and maintained monthly as interim or alternative to HT.
Use ketoconazole 2% shampoo (Nizoral) 2-3x/week as adjunct for scalp health and mild anti-androgen effect; leave on 3-5min.
For beard density maintenance after minox: dermaroll 0.5mm 1x/week + minox; trim to even length that suits jaw.
Laser hair removal or electrolysis for unibrow, neck beard, or unwanted body hair if it distracts (e.g. back, shoulders for some).
If balding aggressively and no transplant candidate yet, buzz cut (#1-2 guard) or shaved head + groomed beard/stubble + tan for "bald by choice" strong look.
Supplement biotin 5-10mg + collagen peptides 10-15g daily only if diet deficient; track if helps nails/hair quality.
Protect hair from sun/heat: hat outdoors, no daily hot tools, use leave-in conditioner or argan oil sparingly.
For curly/wavy: use sulfate-free shampoo, curl cream or gel on damp hair, diffuse or air dry for volume without frizz.
Consider low dose dutasteride 0.5mg 2-3x/week if finasteride not enough after 12mo (off label, discuss with doc).
Get bloods (DHT, testosterone, iron, thyroid, vit D) before starting any hair protocol; address root causes if low T or hypothyroid contributing to shedding.

Complete Invisalign or equivalent clear aligner treatment (or braces) for 12-24 months to fix crowding, overbite/underbite, achieve ideal smile arc with 8-10 upper teeth showing on broad smile and proper lip support.
Professional teeth whitening (in-office or custom trays with 10-16% carbamide peroxide) 1-2x/year + daily maintenance strips or charcoal if needed; avoid over-whitening to unnatural.
Fix gum health: daily flossing + water flosser, electric toothbrush, 2x/year professional cleanings; treat any periodontal issues.
If short teeth or gummy smile, consult for crown lengthening or botox lip flip (temporary) or orthognathic that advances maxilla.
Consider composite bonding or porcelain veneers (minimal prep) for chipped, short, or discolored teeth after alignment and whitening; choose natural proportions.
Practice proper tongue posture and swallowing (mewing + no tongue thrust) during ortho to stabilize results and support palate.
If wisdom teeth impacting or causing crowding, extract under oral surgeon guidance before or during ortho planning.
Maintain fresh breath: tongue scrape daily, stay hydrated, address any tonsil stones or reflux.
For recessed maxilla contributing to flat midface/poor smile support: explore MSE or SARPE palate expansion + ortho if young adult or teen; otherwise surgical options later.
Post ortho or surgery, wear retainers lifelong as prescribed (nightly Vivera or fixed).

Practice full-time mewing (entire tongue pressed/suctioned flat against palate including posterior, teeth in light contact or slightly apart, nasal breathing only, lips together) + correct head posture.
Chew mastic gum or falim 1-2 hours/day (build up) or use Jawliner device for masseter hypertrophy if you have narrow or recessed lower third and want wider jaw angles (monitor for TMJ).
Groom and subtly lift eyebrows: professional shaping or at-home with spoolie + clear gel; if very low set or hooded, consider brow lift or botox brow lift by experienced injector.
For negative canthal tilt or hollow under eyes: consistent skincare (tret + vit C + peptides), sleep, allergy control, filler in tear troughs by expert (0.5-1ml HA carefully).
Build or maintain slight positive canthal tilt illusion via makeup-free methods for men: slight upward brow tilt, good upper eyelid exposure via fat loss or bleph if severe.
Tan strategically (sunless with DHA lotion or controlled UVB) to even skin tone, sharpen features, and add contrast; never burn.
If buccal fat pads make face round/puffy despite low BF: consult for buccal fat removal (small intraoral procedure); only if truly excess, not just high BF.
Get professional lash lift/tint or use castor oil/serum nightly for longer lashes if short (subtle masculine enhancement).
Reduce masseter if overly square/hypertrophied face with masseter botox (20-40 units/side every 4-6mo) by injector experienced with male faces.
For recessed chin without full jaw issues: 1-2ml HA filler or chin implant (silicone or sliding genio) for projection and profile balance.
Fix ear position if protruding: otoplasty (ear pinning) under local or general; high satisfaction for side profile.
Nose: if large hump or bulbous, rhinoplasty with experienced surgeon focusing on masculine straight or slight aquiline profile, not feminized.
Cheekbones: if flat, subtle HA filler or fat transfer (not overdone); or accept and enhance with lighting/contour/lower BF.
Lip: if very thin upper, 0.5-1ml HA for subtle projection (male appropriate, avoid duck).

Overall face harmony: use free or paid AI facial analysis tools (as discussed in LooksmaxingAdvice cheat sheet) on multiple well-lit neutral photos from front/side/45deg for baseline and tracking progress.

If cephalometrics/CBCT show true maxillary/mandibular retrognathia, negative overjet, or compromised airway: pursue bimaxillary osteotomy (DJS/bimax) + genioplasty with top-tier maxillofacial surgeon after 12-18mo ortho decompensation.
Hair transplant (FUE) as detailed in #32.
Chin/jaw angle implants or custom PEEK implants for permanent lower third projection and width if filler not enough or desired permanent.
Rhinoplasty as #67.
Blepharoplasty (upper/lower lid) or canthoplasty/canthopexy for hooded, negative tilt, or scleral show correction.
Buccal fat removal as #62 if indicated.
For severe gynecomastia or chest fat that won't budge: gland excision + lipo by specialist.
Liposuction or body contouring (abdomen, love handles, submental) after reaching ~12-14% to remove stubborn pockets.
If leg length discrepancy or very short stature and motivated: consider limb lengthening surgery (Precice or similar) with experienced surgeon; 5-8cm typical.
Fat transfer (microfat or nano) to under eyes, temples, or cheeks for youthful volume if hollow from low BF or genetics.
For aggressive hair loss in transplant candidate: combine HT with PRP or exosomes adjunct if clinic offers.
Jaw botox or filler for temporary angle definition while deciding on implants.
Midface implants or deep plane facelift elements if significant sagging or flat (rare in young men).
Voice? No, skip non-looks; focus only physical.
Get full blood panel + hormone optimization (TRT if clinically low T with symptoms, under doctor) before/ during major protocols; low T hinders muscle, skin, hair, mood, energy.

Build a 10-15 piece capsule wardrobe of well-fitting (tailored if needed) items: dark straight or slim jeans/chinos, oxford shirts, merino crewnecks, unstructured blazer, quality leather sneakers/boots, white tees, one overcoat.
Apply fragrance correctly: 2 sprays max (neck base + chest or wrist), on clean skin post-moisturizer; choose fresh, woody, or clean scents that get compliments in your demographic (test on skin).
Shoes: rotate 3-4 pairs of clean, well-maintained, context-appropriate footwear (white sneakers, chelsea, loafers, boots); match belt to shoes.
Nails, hands, neck: keep nails short/clean, moisturize hands, shave or trim neck hair cleanly below beard line or full clean shave if no beard.
Color coordinate: choose clothing colors that flatter your skin undertone/hair/eyes (cool/warm analysis via apps or pro); avoid clashing.
Posture in motion: shoulders back/down, chest up, chin parallel to ground, relaxed arms; practice walking tall in mirror or recorded video.
Watch/jewelry: one quality simple watch (leather or metal band appropriate to outfit), no more than one ring/bracelet unless cultural; subtle status without flash.
Underwear and base layers that fit without lines or sagging; good quality socks that don't show ankle skin.
No smoking/vaping, limit alcohol to <7 drinks/week, minimize ultra-processed food.
Stress management: daily 10min meditation or breathwork, nature time, social hobbies; high chronic cortisol shows in face (puffiness, lines, fat distribution).
Collagen or bone broth daily (15-20g) + adequate vitamin C for skin elasticity support during cut or post surgery.
Do chin tucks (3x10-15, hold 5-10s) + chin curls or plate neck work 3-4x per week to correct forward head posture and support jawline projection.
Cold exposure: end showers with 30-60s cold, or ice face rolling 2-3x/week for temporary de-puff and circulation (supplement not replace skincare).
Document progress with consistent lighting/angle/pose photos every 4 weeks (front, side, 45, smile, neutral); use same camera/time of day.
